column2: A SOHO workstation uses 192.168.50.20 internally and reaches the internet through its router. Is the workstation address public or private?
column5:
Why

The 192.168.0.0/16 block is reserved for private networks and is not routed directly across the public internet.

Exam Trap

Internet access does not make the workstation's local address public. A SOHO router commonly uses NAT so private IPv4 hosts can share its public-facing connection when accessing public networks.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.17; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.6

column2: An IP address is written as hexadecimal groups separated by colons. Which IP version uses this format?
column5:
Exam Trap

IPv4 normally appears as four decimal octets separated by periods; IPv6 uses hexadecimal and colons.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.17; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.6
column10: A+::220-1201::2.6 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::BasicSOHONetworkConfiguration Basic IPv6 AddressFormat Source::Messer-v170

column2: A policy requires a technician to enter a device's IP settings directly and keep them unchanged until an administrator edits them. Is this static or dynamic addressing?
column5:
Why

Manually entered settings remain fixed until they are changed. Dynamic settings are supplied automatically and may change.

Real Help Desk

Manual static settings require careful maintenance because later network changes must also be entered on the device.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.18; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.6
column10: A+::220-1201::2.6 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::BasicSOHONetworkConfiguration Basic StaticAddressing DynamicAddressing Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A host has the correct IPv4 address but incorrectly decides whether destinations are local or require routing. Which IP setting should be checked first?
column5:
Why

The subnet mask tells the host which addresses belong to its local subnet.

Exam Trap

The default gateway handles off-subnet forwarding, but the mask determines whether the host considers a destination off-subnet in the first place.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.18; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.6
column10: A+::220-1201::2.6 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::BasicSOHONetworkConfiguration Basic HighYield SubnetMask Troubleshooting Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A workstation can reach devices on its local subnet but cannot reach any off-subnet destination. Its IP address and subnet mask are correct. Which setting should be checked first?
column5:
Why

The default gateway is the local router address used to send traffic beyond the host's subnet.

Exam Trap

The gateway must be reachable on the local subnet; an unrelated or mistyped address cannot provide the off-subnet path.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.18; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.6
column10: A+::220-1201::2.6 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::BasicSOHONetworkConfiguration Basic HighYield Gateway Troubleshooting Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A workstation configured for automatic addressing assigns itself a 169.254.x.x address and cannot reach routed networks. What does this address indicate?
column4: The workstation could not obtain an address from DHCP and used APIPA.
column5:
Why

APIPA provides local link communication when automatic DHCP configuration is unavailable.

Exam Trap

An APIPA address is a symptom to investigate DHCP reachability; it is not a normal routed address for internet access.
